steps: Open your week's playlist file — the one your local-music host already has.
Put it in a folder on your computer and open Claude Code there.
Ask Claude Code to read the playlist and, for each local artist, search online for a short bio, upcoming tour dates, and any recent release news.
Ask it to put all of that into one prep sheet, organized by artist, in the order they're playing.
Read the prep sheet and flag anything that sounds off or too good to be true.
Before your host says anything on air about a tour date, label, or hometown, check it against a real source — the artist's own site or a wire story.
verify: Pick three claims from the prep sheet — a tour date, a label, a hometown — and look each one up yourself on the artist's own site before your host reads any of them on air.
